mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals an imminent labor market contraction, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of triggered instances or trigger fails to predict direction with accuracy >52%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising retail sales and falling housing starts indicates structural demand weakness in the housing sector, dragging down equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression or predictive accuracy drops below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production confirms robust manufacturing output and capacity utilization, supporting corporate earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal reverses direction during late-cycle phases or fails to predict returns with Rank IC > 0.04 in recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 200 signal entrenched inflationary pressures, forcing markets to price in prolonged restrictive policy and negative equity returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Returns remain positive in >60% of threshold-crossing instances or signal accuracy falls below 51%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market, boosting consumer spending expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to outperform baseline or interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects broad-based economic expansion, directly supporting corporate revenue forecasts and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window or positive return frequency drops below 55% during growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a credit tightening phase signals supply chain bottlenecks and reduced factory output, triggering immediate sell-offs and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) during credit expansion periods or fails to trigger negative returns in >55% of cases.
